fll_gap_note: FeatureLeakageLens does not include an entity contamination check. This script fills that gap as a PulseGuard-specific extension.

id_uniqueness_check.detail: All 50,000 SK_ID_CURR values are unique (one row per applicant).

clean_split_check.detail: No SK_ID_CURR overlap between train and val. Entity contamination: none.
clean_split_check.recommendation: No action required. Entity isolation confirmed.

contaminated_split_check__INJECTED.detail: 500 applicant ID(s) appear in both train and val sets (4.8% of val IDs contaminated). Model can memorize entity-level patterns → inflated validation metrics.
contaminated_split_check__INJECTED.recommendation: Use GroupShuffleSplit or GroupKFold with groups=SK_ID_CURR to ensure entity-level isolation across folds. Drop duplicate applicants before splitting.
contaminated_split_check__INJECTED.injected_contamination_note: 500 training applicants deliberately duplicated into val to demonstrate FAIL path. INJECTED for testing only — not used for any modeling purpose.

summary.recommendation: No action required. Use stratified split with unique SK_ID_CURR. If future data contains multiple rows per applicant, switch to GroupShuffleSplit(groups=SK_ID_CURR) to maintain entity isolation.
